tasks.title: iOS Feature Parity Audit & Gap Closure
tasks.description: Audit the iOS app against the web app to identify all missing features, then implement the critical gaps. The iOS app has core screens (login, register, feed, profile, graph, discovery, notifications, settings, compose) but is missing many web features including: marketplace, direct messages, bookmarks list, admin dashboard, password reset, email verification UI, leaderboard, submolts, MCP tools, webhooks, transaction history, and analytics tracking (trackEvent calls). Priority: add analytics tracking to existing iOS screens and build out the most impactful missing features.
tasks.details:
## iOS App Current State (28 Swift files)

Implemented screens: LoginView, RegisterView, FeedView, DiscoveryView, GraphView, ProfileView, ProfileDetailView, NotificationsView, SettingsView, ComposePostView

Implemented services: APIService, KeychainService, EnvironmentConfig
ViewModels: Auth, Feed, Discovery, Graph, Notifications, Profile
Components: GlassCard, PostCard, EntityRow, LoadingStateView, Theme, DateFormatting

## Critical Gaps (Priority Order)

1. **Analytics Tracking** — Add trackEvent calls matching web analytics.ts pattern:
   - LoginView: track login_start, login_complete
   - RegisterView: track register_start, register_complete (already has backend support)
   - FeedView: track guest_page_view, guest_cta_click
   - All guest CTA prompts: track guest_cta_click with intent
   - POST to /api/v1/analytics/event (same endpoint as web)
   - Use UUID stored in UserDefaults as session_id

2. **Bookmarks View** — iOS can bookmark posts (FeedViewModel has bookmarking) but no screen to view bookmarks

3. **Password Reset Flow** — ForgotPasswordView + ResetPasswordView (web has both)

4. **Email Verification UI** — VerifyEmailView to handle email verification links

5. **Leaderboard** — Trust/posts/followers rankings

6. **Submolts** — Browse and view submolt communities

7. **Marketplace** — Browse listings, listing detail, create listing (Phase 2 but web has it)

8. **Direct Messages** — Messaging between entities

9. **Dashboard** — User dashboard with stats

10. **Settings Enhancement** — Full settings (email/password change, notification preferences, privacy)

## Lower Priority (Developer Features)
- MCP Tools browser
- Webhook management
- Transaction history
- Admin dashboard (may not be needed on mobile)

## Implementation Notes
- Follow existing SwiftUI patterns in ios/AgentGraph/Sources/
- Use APIService.swift for all network calls
- Match Liquid Glass design language
- Test on iOS 18+ simulator
tasks.testStrategy: Build project with xcodegen after each feature addition. Verify new screens render in simulator. Test analytics events appear in admin conversion dashboard. Verify navigation flow between new and existing screens. Test all API calls succeed against local dev server.

tasks.title: Trust Score v2 — Community Attestations & Contextual Trust
tasks.description: Enhance trust scoring with community attestations, contextual trust scores, and gaming prevention. Add TrustAttestation model, update algorithm to 5 components (community 20%), add per-context scores, attestation CRUD endpoints, and decay/cap mechanisms.
tasks.details:
Backend:
1. New TrustAttestation model (src/models.py): id, attester_entity_id, target_entity_id, attestation_type (competent|reliable|safe|responsive), context (string), weight (float 0-1 from attester trust), comment, created_at. Unique on (attester, target, type).
2. Update trust algorithm (src/trust/score.py): add 5th component 'community' (20% weight). New weights: verification 35%, activity 20%, reputation 15%, community 20%, age 10%. Community = avg attestation weight from other entities.
3. Add contextual_scores JSONB to trust_scores table — per-context scores (e.g. {"code_review": 0.85}).
4. Gaming prevention: cap 10 attestations per attester per target, decay >90 days (50% weight), >180 days (25% weight).
5. New endpoints in trust_router.py:
   - POST /entities/{id}/attestations — create (can't self-attest)
   - GET /entities/{id}/attestations — list received
   - GET /entities/{id}/trust/contextual?context=X — contextual score
   - DELETE /entities/{id}/attestations/{att_id} — revoke
6. Alembic migration: trust_attestations table + contextual_scores column
7. MCP tools: agentgraph_attest_entity, agentgraph_list_attestations
8. Web: update TrustDetail.tsx with community component + contextual scores + attestation list
9. iOS: add attestation list to ProfileDetailView trust section
10. Tests: attestation CRUD, contextual scoring, gaming cap, decay, v2 weights
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test attestation model constraints (unique, no self-attest). Test v2 algorithm produces expected scores with attestation data. Test gaming cap (11th attestation rejected). Test decay (verify 91-day-old attestation weighted at 50%). Test contextual scoring filters correctly. Integration test full flow: create attestation → recompute trust → verify score increased.

tasks.title: Privacy Tier Enforcement on Feed, Posts, Evolution & Graph
tasks.description: Extend privacy tier enforcement beyond profiles/search to cover feed posts, replies, evolution records, trust scores, and graph data. PRIVATE-tier entities should be invisible to non-followers across all surfaces.
tasks.details:
Backend:
1. Feed privacy (src/api/feed_router.py):
   - GET /feed/posts: exclude posts from PRIVATE-tier authors unless requester follows them
   - GET /feed/posts/{id}: check author privacy tier, return 403 for PRIVATE if not follower
   - GET /feed/posts/{id}/replies: filter replies from PRIVATE authors
2. Evolution privacy (src/api/evolution_router.py):
   - GET /evolution/{entity_id}: check entity privacy tier before returning timeline
3. Trust privacy (src/api/trust_router.py):
   - GET /entities/{id}/trust: respect privacy tier (PRIVATE = 403 unless follower)
4. Graph privacy (src/api/graph_router.py):
   - Exclude PRIVATE entities from graph export and ego-graph unless requester is connected
5. Notification privacy:
   - Don't reveal PRIVATE entity display names in notification titles to non-followers
6. Helper function: _check_privacy_access(target_entity, requester_entity, db) → bool
   - PUBLIC: always True
   - VERIFIED: requester email_verified or has DID
   - PRIVATE: requester follows target (check entity_relationships)
7. No migration needed (privacy_tier column exists)
8. Web: add privacy tier selector to Settings page
9. iOS: add privacy tier picker to SettingsView
10. Tests: 15+ new tests covering feed filtering, post access, evolution access, graph exclusion, notification masking
tasks.testStrategy: Create PRIVATE entity with posts. Verify unauthenticated user cannot see posts in feed. Verify non-follower gets 403 on direct post access. Verify follower CAN see posts. Test evolution endpoint returns 403 for PRIVATE entity. Test graph export excludes PRIVATE nodes. Test notification title shows "Someone" instead of display name for PRIVATE entities.

tasks.title: Marketplace Payment Integration — Stripe Connect
tasks.description: Integrate Stripe Connect for marketplace payments. Sellers onboard via Stripe Connect, buyers pay via PaymentIntents, platform takes 10% fee. Handle webhooks for payment confirmation and refunds.
tasks.details:
Backend:
1. New service: src/payments/stripe_service.py
   - create_connect_account(entity_id) — create Stripe Connect Express account
   - create_onboarding_link(account_id, return_url) — return onboarding URL
   - create_payment_intent(amount_cents, seller_account_id, platform_fee_cents) — create PI with transfer
   - handle_webhook(payload, signature) — process Stripe webhook events
   - get_account_status(account_id) — check if seller can accept payments
2. Model changes:
   - Entity: add stripe_account_id (String, nullable)
   - Transaction: add stripe_payment_intent_id, stripe_transfer_id, platform_fee_cents
3. Updated/new endpoints (marketplace_router.py):
   - POST /marketplace/connect/onboard — initiate seller onboarding, return URL
   - GET /marketplace/connect/status — seller's Stripe account status
   - POST /marketplace/{id}/purchase — UPDATED: create PaymentIntent for paid listings, return client_secret
   - POST /marketplace/stripe/webhook — handle payment_intent.succeeded, charge.refunded events
4. Payment flow: buyer calls purchase → gets client_secret → Stripe.js confirms → webhook marks transaction COMPLETED → platform fee split
5. Free listings: continue auto-completing (no Stripe needed)
6. Alembic migration: add stripe columns
7. pip install stripe
8. Web: Stripe Elements on ListingDetail for checkout, seller onboarding in Settings
9. iOS: web-based checkout sheet (SFSafariViewController) or Stripe iOS SDK

.env.secrets (REQUIRED):
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_xxx
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_xxx

.env (OPTIONAL):
STRIPE_PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT=10
tasks.testStrategy: Mock Stripe API with unittest.mock. Test Connect account creation. Test PaymentIntent creation with correct amount and fee split. Test webhook signature verification. Test payment_intent.succeeded webhook marks transaction COMPLETED. Test charge.refunded webhook marks transaction REFUNDED. Test free listings still auto-complete without Stripe. Test seller without Stripe account gets 400 on paid listing creation.

tasks.title: Formal Attestation & Verification Badge Framework
tasks.description: Add structured verification badges (email_verified, identity_verified, capability_audited, agentgraph_verified) and formal audit records. Auto-issue system badges on verification events. Enforce endorsement tier requirements.
tasks.details:
Backend:
1. New VerificationBadge model (src/models.py): id, entity_id, badge_type (email_verified|identity_verified|capability_audited|agentgraph_verified), issued_by (entity_id nullable for system), proof_url (nullable), expires_at (nullable), is_active, created_at
2. New AuditRecord model: id, target_entity_id, auditor_entity_id, audit_type (security|capability|compliance), result (pass|fail|partial), findings (JSONB), report_url, created_at
3. Auto-issue system badges:
   - email_verified badge auto-created when email verification succeeds (update auth router)
   - identity_verified badge when DID document is registered
4. Endorsement tier enforcement (update CapabilityEndorsement logic):
   - community_verified: requires 3+ attestations from entities with trust > 0.5
   - formally_audited: requires AuditRecord with result=pass for that capability
5. New endpoints:
   - GET /entities/{id}/badges — list verification badges
   - POST /entities/{id}/badges (admin only) — manually issue badge
   - POST /entities/{id}/audit — submit audit record (verified auditors only)
   - GET /entities/{id}/audit-history — audit trail
6. Migration: verification_badges + audit_records tables
7. Web: badge icons on profiles, audit history section
8. iOS: badge icons row on ProfileDetailView
tasks.testStrategy: Test auto-badge on email verification. Test admin badge issuance. Test non-admin cannot issue badges. Test endorsement tier enforcement (community_verified requires 3 attestations from high-trust entities). Test audit record creation and linkage. Test badge expiry (expired badge excluded from active list). Test badge display in profile response.

tasks.title: OpenClaw Bridge — Second Framework Adapter with Security Scanning
tasks.description: Build OpenClaw framework bridge following MCP bridge pattern. Add security scanning for imported agents, malicious pattern detection, framework trust modifier, and vulnerability database checking.
tasks.details:
Backend:
1. New module: src/bridges/openclaw/
   - adapter.py — translate OpenClaw skill definitions to AgentGraph capability format
   - security.py — scan skill code for malicious patterns (prompt injection regex, command injection, known CVE patterns)
   - registry.py — import OpenClaw agent manifest → create Entity + capabilities + evolution record
2. New FrameworkSecurityScan model (src/models.py): id, entity_id, framework (mcp|openclaw|langchain), scan_result (clean|warnings|critical), vulnerabilities (JSONB array), scanned_at
3. Entity model additions: framework_source (nullable string: mcp|openclaw|langchain), framework_trust_modifier (float, default 1.0)
4. Security scanning logic:
   - Regex patterns for prompt injection, command injection, eval/exec usage
   - Check against known malicious skill hashes (configurable blocklist)
   - Severity classification: info, warning, critical
   - Block import if any critical findings
5. Framework trust modifier:
   - OpenClaw agents start with 0.8x trust modifier (due to ecosystem CVE history)
   - Clean security scan removes penalty (1.0x)
   - Critical scan drops to 0.5x
   - Modifier applied in trust score computation
6. New endpoints (src/api/bridges_router.py):
   - POST /bridges/openclaw/import — scan + register agent
   - GET /bridges/openclaw/scan/{entity_id} — latest scan result
   - POST /bridges/openclaw/rescan/{entity_id} — trigger rescan
   - GET /bridges/status — supported frameworks + agent counts per framework
7. Migration: framework_security_scans table + entity columns
8. Tests: import flow, scan with clean fixture, scan with vulnerable fixture, trust modifier, rescan
tasks.testStrategy: Test clean agent import (no vulnerabilities). Test vulnerable agent blocked on import (critical finding). Test warning-level findings allow import with reduced trust. Test trust modifier applied correctly in score computation. Test rescan updates scan result and trust modifier. Test /bridges/status returns correct counts. Mock OpenClaw manifest format for import tests.

tasks.title: Scheduled Trust Recomputation & Attestation Decay
tasks.description: Add daily background job for batch trust recomputation with attestation decay, activity recency weighting, and admin stats endpoint.
tasks.details:
Backend:
1. New module: src/jobs/trust_recompute.py
   - async def recompute_all_trust_scores(db): iterate all active entities, recompute trust
   - Apply attestation decay: >90 days = 50% weight, >180 days = 25% weight, >365 days = 10%
   - Apply activity recency weighting: last 30 days = 100%, 30-90 days = 50%, >90 days = 25%
   - Log stats: entities_processed, scores_changed (delta > 0.1), avg_score, computation_time_ms
2. Scheduler integration:
   - Option A: APScheduler (pip install apscheduler) with daily trigger
   - Option B: FastAPI lifespan + asyncio.create_task with sleep loop
   - Run at 3:00 AM UTC daily
3. Admin endpoints:
   - POST /admin/trust/recompute-all — trigger manual batch recompute (already partially exists, enhance)
   - GET /admin/trust/stats — trust distribution: histogram (0-0.2, 0.2-0.4, etc.), avg by entity type, total entities scored
4. Update batch_recompute() in trust/score.py to use decay + recency
5. Tests: verify decay calculation at 91 days, 181 days. Verify recency weighting. Verify batch processes all entities. Verify stats endpoint returns correct histogram.
tasks.testStrategy: Create entities with attestations at various ages (30, 91, 181 days ago). Run batch recompute. Verify 91-day attestation weighted at 50%. Create entities with old-only activity vs recent activity. Verify recent activity scored higher. Test admin stats returns correct histogram buckets. Test manual trigger via POST /admin/trust/recompute-all.

tasks.title: Deep Dive Security & Performance Audit
tasks.description: Thorough security and performance review across all 4 phases. Audit authentication flows, authorization checks, input validation, SQL injection vectors, rate limiting effectiveness, CORS config, and OWASP Top 10 compliance. Profile slow queries, identify N+1 problems, and benchmark key endpoints under load.
tasks.details:
Three-pronged audit:

1. Security Audit:
   - Auth: JWT token handling, refresh flow, password reset, session management
   - Authorization: Role checks on all admin/org endpoints, entity ownership validation
   - Input validation: All request bodies, query params, path params
   - SQL injection: Raw SQL usage in analytics/insights queries
   - XSS: Content sanitization in posts, listings, DMs
   - CORS: Verify allowlist is correct for all environments
   - Rate limiting: Verify all write endpoints are rate-limited
   - Secrets: No hardcoded keys, proper .env.secrets usage
   - Dependencies: Check for known CVEs in pip packages

2. Performance Audit:
   - Profile top 20 endpoints with realistic data volume
   - Identify missing indexes (EXPLAIN ANALYZE on slow queries)
   - Check N+1 query patterns (especially feed, marketplace browse)
   - Redis cache hit rates and TTL effectiveness
   - WebSocket connection scaling
   - Database connection pool sizing

3. Code Quality:
   - Dead code identification
   - Consistent error handling patterns
   - Proper async/await usage (no blocking calls in async context)
   - Test coverage gaps

Output: Security findings report with severity ratings, performance bottleneck list with recommendations, code quality issues.
tasks.testStrategy: Run automated security scan (bandit, safety). Profile endpoints with wrk/hey. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on all analytics queries. Check test coverage report. Verify all findings are actionable.

tasks.title: Load Testing — Stress Test API at Scale
tasks.description: Design and execute comprehensive load tests against the AgentGraph API. Identify breaking points, bottlenecks, and scalability limits. Test with realistic traffic patterns simulating target user counts (1K, 10K, 100K entities).
tasks.details:
Load testing strategy:

1. Tool Selection:
   - k6 (preferred) or Locust for HTTP load testing
   - Custom WebSocket load test script
   - PostgreSQL pgbench for database stress

2. Test Scenarios:
   a. Feed browsing (GET /feed with pagination) — highest traffic endpoint
   b. Authentication flow (register + login + token refresh)
   c. Post creation with content filtering
   d. Marketplace browse + search
   e. Trust score computation under concurrent writes
   f. WebSocket connection storm (1000+ concurrent)
   g. Insights endpoints under cache miss conditions
   h. AIP delegation creation burst
   i. Mixed realistic traffic pattern (70% read, 20% write, 10% search)

3. Scale Targets:
   - 100 concurrent users: should handle easily
   - 1,000 concurrent: identify first bottlenecks
   - 10,000 concurrent: find breaking point
   - Sustained 1K req/s for 30 minutes

4. Metrics to Capture:
   - p50, p95, p99 latency per endpoint
   - Throughput (req/s)
   - Error rate
   - Database connection pool utilization
   - Redis cache hit rate
   - Memory and CPU usage
   - WebSocket message delivery latency

5. Output:
   - Performance baseline document
   - Bottleneck analysis with fix recommendations
   - Scaling recommendations (horizontal vs vertical)
   - Cost projection at target scale
tasks.testStrategy: Run load tests on staging environment (Docker Compose). Compare results against SLA targets. Verify no data corruption under load. Check for connection leaks. Verify graceful degradation under overload.

tasks.title: iOS Graph — Interactive Node Dragging & Advanced Interactions
tasks.description: Enhance the iOS SwiftUI Canvas force graph with interactive node manipulation: long-press and drag to reposition individual nodes, double-tap to center on a node, pinch-on-node to expand/collapse neighbors, and haptic feedback for interactions.
tasks.details:
Enhance ForceGraphView.swift (SwiftUI Canvas) with advanced interactions:

1. Long-Press & Drag to Reposition Nodes:
   - Long press on a node (0.5s) to 'grab' it — show haptic feedback + visual highlight
   - While holding, drag to reposition the node in graph space
   - On release, the node stays at the new position (pin it)
   - Other nodes should re-simulate around the pinned node
   - Requires converting the simple force layout to an incremental simulation
   - Must coexist with existing pan gesture (drag on empty space = pan, drag on node = move node)

2. Double-Tap to Center & Zoom:
   - Double-tap a node to animate zoom + pan so the node is centered at 2x zoom
   - Useful for navigating large graphs

3. Node Context Menu (Long Press without Drag):
   - If user long-presses but doesn't drag, show a context menu:
     - View Profile
     - View Trust Flow
     - Center on This Node (ego-graph reload)
     - Copy Node ID

4. Edge Interaction:
   - Tap on an edge to show a popover with edge type + weight
   - Requires edge hit-testing (closest point on line segment)

5. Incremental Force Simulation:
   - Replace the current 80-iteration batch simulation with a live simulation
   - Use CADisplayLink or Timer to run simulation steps continuously
   - Allow nodes to settle naturally with velocity damping
   - Pinned/dragged nodes have fixed positions, others simulate freely
   - Stop simulation when all velocities fall below threshold

6. Gesture Architecture:
   - LongPressGesture(minimumDuration: 0.5) → DragGesture for node dragging
   - TapGesture(count: 2) for double-tap center
   - Existing MagnifyGesture for zoom
   - Existing DragGesture(minimumDistance: 10) for pan
   - Must handle gesture priority correctly (node drag > pan)

7. Visual Feedback:
   - Grabbed node: pulsing ring + slight scale-up
   - Dragging: node follows finger with subtle shadow
   - Pinned nodes: small pin icon or different border style
   - Haptic feedback: UIImpactFeedbackGenerator on grab/release

8. Performance:
   - Incremental simulation should maintain 60fps on iPhone 12+
   - Use spatial hashing for O(1) node hit-testing instead of linear scan
   - Limit simulation to visible nodes when graph > 200 nodes
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual testing on iOS Simulator and device:
1. Long-press a node — verify haptic fires and visual highlight appears
2. Drag a held node — verify it follows finger and other nodes re-simulate
3. Release — verify node stays pinned at new position
4. Double-tap a node — verify smooth animated center + zoom
5. Long-press without drag — verify context menu appears
6. Verify pan still works when starting on empty space
7. Verify zoom still works with pinch gesture
8. Test with 50+ nodes — verify no frame drops during drag
9. Test gesture conflicts — ensure no accidental node drags when panning

tasks.description: Replace allow_methods=['*'] and allow_headers=['*'] in CORS middleware with explicit lists. Add production origin (https://agentgraph.io) to cors_origins. File: src/main.py.
tasks.details:
Security hardening for production CORS configuration in src/main.py.

Current state: CORS middleware uses allow_methods=['*'] and allow_headers=['*'] which is overly permissive.

Changes needed:
1. Replace allow_methods=['*'] with explicit list:
   allow_methods=['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS']

2. Replace allow_headers=['*'] with explicit list:
   allow_headers=['Authorization', 'Content-Type', 'Accept', 'Origin', 'X-Requested-With']

3. Add production origin to cors_origins:
   - https://agentgraph.io
   - https://www.agentgraph.io
   - Keep existing dev/staging origins for non-production environments

4. Ensure allow_credentials=True is set (needed for cookie-based auth)

5. Test that:
   - Frontend can still make API calls from all environments
   - Preflight OPTIONS requests work correctly
   - WebSocket upgrade requests are not blocked
   - iOS app requests work (no Origin header)
tasks.testStrategy: Verify frontend API calls work from dev (:5173), staging (:5174), and production origins. Test preflight OPTIONS requests return correct headers. Verify WebSocket connections still work. Test iOS app requests succeed without Origin header. Confirm wildcard methods/headers are no longer present.

tasks.title: WebSocket Auth — Move Token from URL to First Message
tasks.description: Update frontend WebSocket hook (web/src/hooks/useWebSocket.tsx) to send JWT token as the first message after connection instead of in the URL query parameter. URL query params leak in server logs and referrer headers. Backend ws_router.py already supports both methods.
tasks.details:
Security improvement: JWT tokens in WebSocket URL query params are logged by nginx, appear in server access logs, and can leak via Referrer headers.

Backend (src/api/ws_router.py) already supports token-as-first-message pattern — no backend changes needed.

Frontend changes needed in web/src/hooks/useWebSocket.tsx:
1. Remove token from WebSocket URL construction:
   - Before: new WebSocket(`ws://host/ws?token=${accessToken}`)
   - After: new WebSocket(`ws://host/ws`)

2. Send token as first message after connection opens:
   - ws.onopen = () => { ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'auth', token: accessToken })) }

3. Handle auth response:
   - Wait for server auth confirmation before considering connection ready
   - Handle auth failure (invalid/expired token) gracefully

4. Update reconnection logic to re-send auth on reconnect

5. Update WSS URL for production (wss:// instead of ws://)

This is a frontend-only change.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify WebSocket connects and authenticates via first-message token. Confirm token no longer appears in URL. Test reconnection sends auth again. Test expired token handling. Verify nginx access logs no longer contain JWT tokens. Test on staging.

tasks.title: Web Token Refresh — Auto-refresh on 401
tasks.description: Add HTTP interceptor in web/src/lib/api.ts that catches 401 responses, calls POST /auth/refresh with the refresh token, queues failed requests, and retries them with the new access token. Store refresh token alongside access token.
tasks.details:
Implement transparent token refresh to prevent users from being logged out when access tokens expire (15 min TTL).

Changes needed in web/src/lib/api.ts:
1. Store refresh token in localStorage alongside access token on login
2. Add response interceptor that catches 401 errors:
   a. Pause all outgoing requests (queue them)
   b. Call POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with refresh_token
   c. On success: update stored access token, retry all queued requests
   d. On failure: clear tokens, redirect to /login
3. Prevent multiple simultaneous refresh attempts (use a lock/promise)
4. Handle edge cases:
   - Refresh token also expired — redirect to login
   - Network error during refresh — retry with backoff
   - Race condition: multiple 401s trigger single refresh

The backend /auth/refresh endpoint already exists and works.

Note: Per MEMORY.md, this may already be partially implemented ('Token refresh: web/src/lib/api.ts auto-refreshes on 401 with queue'). Verify current state before making changes.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify 401 triggers automatic token refresh. Confirm queued requests retry with new token. Test concurrent 401s only trigger one refresh. Test expired refresh token redirects to login. Test network error during refresh. Verify on staging.

tasks.title: Daily Backup Cron Setup
tasks.description: Add crontab entry on MacBook for scripts/backup-prod.sh to run daily at 3 AM. MANUAL task — user must configure the cron job.
tasks.details:
MANUAL TASK — User must add crontab entry.

The backup script (scripts/backup-prod.sh) should already exist and handle:
- SSH into EC2 instance
- Run pg_dump on production PostgreSQL
- Download the backup file
- Store locally with date-stamped filename
- Optionally upload to S3 or other remote storage
- Clean up old backups (keep last 30 days)

Steps:
1. Verify scripts/backup-prod.sh exists and works manually:
   bash scripts/backup-prod.sh
2. Make executable: chmod +x scripts/backup-prod.sh
3. Add crontab entry:
   crontab -e
   0 3 * * * /Users/kserver/projects/agentgraph/scripts/backup-prod.sh >> /Users/kserver/projects/agentgraph/logs/backup.log 2>&1
4. Verify crontab was saved:
   crontab -l
5. Wait for next 3 AM run and check logs
6. Verify backup file was created and is valid:
   pg_restore --list <backup_file>

Alternative: Use launchd plist instead of cron on macOS for better reliability.
tasks.testStrategy: Run backup script manually and verify it completes successfully. Check backup file is valid with pg_restore --list. After cron is set, verify next morning that backup ran. Test restore to agentgraph_test database.

tasks.title: Bidirectional Attestation Ring Detection
tasks.description: Implement detection of mutual attestation rings and voting cartels — the simplest and most dangerous collusion pattern. When Agent A attests to Agent B AND B attests to A, flag both for review. Extend to clique detection (3+ entities all attesting to each other) and voting ring detection (coordinated upvote patterns).
tasks.details:
CRITICAL SAFETY — Paper §3.5: cooperation ≠ alignment. Agents can collude.

Files: src/safety/collusion.py (new), src/jobs/collusion_scan.py (new), src/api/anomaly_router.py, src/api/admin_router.py

Requirements:
- New CollusionAlert model (or extend AnomalyAlert): entity_a_id, entity_b_id, alert_type ('mutual_attestation', 'voting_ring', 'attestation_cluster'), severity, details JSONB, is_resolved
- Query trust_attestations for bidirectional pairs: WHERE (attester=A, target=B) AND (attester=B, target=A)
- Clique detection: groups of 3+ entities all mutually attesting (build adjacency graph from attestations, find dense subgraphs)
- Voting rings: detect entities that consistently upvote each other's posts (>80% overlap in voting targets)
- Admin API: GET /admin/collusion/alerts with filtering by type, severity, resolved
- Daily scheduled job on batch recompute
- Auto-flag entities in detected rings for moderation review
- Include collusion stats in admin dashboard

IMPORTANT: Flag for review, don't auto-ban. Legitimate collaborators may mutually attest. Human review required.
tasks.testStrategy: Create 2 entities that mutually attest → verify alert created. Create 4-entity clique → verify cluster detected. Create voting ring (3 entities always upvoting each other) → detected. Verify legitimate single attestation doesn't trigger. Verify admin endpoint returns alerts with proper filtering.

- Indexes: (entity_a_id, entity_b_id, created_at), (entity_b_id, entity_a_id, created_at)

- GET /interactions/{entity_id}/summary — top partners with counts + last interaction
- GET /interactions/{entity_a}/{entity_b}/stats — frequency, type breakdown, first/last dates

Privacy: respect both entities' privacy tiers.

tasks.title: Population Composition Monitoring
tasks.description: Monitor ecosystem population composition to detect Sybil attacks, framework monoculture, and operator flooding. Paper §5 Q2: adversarial actors could flood the ecosystem with agents designed to exploit cooperative equilibria.
tasks.details:
Paper §5 Q2: Who controls population composition? Open ecosystems are vulnerable to flooding.

Files: src/safety/population.py (new), src/jobs/population_scan.py (new), src/api/admin_router.py

New PopulationAlert model:
- alert_type: framework_monoculture, operator_flood, registration_spike, sybil_cluster
- severity, details JSONB, created_at, is_resolved

Detectors:
1. Framework monoculture: alert if any single framework_source > 70% of active agents
2. Operator flood: alert if any single operator registers >10 agents in 24 hours
3. Registration spike: alert if new agent registrations > 3x the 30-day daily average
4. Sybil cluster: alert if >5 new entities share same IP in 24h (log registration IP in entity metadata)

Admin endpoints:
- GET /admin/population/composition — framework distribution, human/agent ratio, top operators by agent count
- GET /admin/population/alerts — alerts with filtering

Hourly scheduled job runs all detectors.
Include population health in admin dashboard.
tasks.testStrategy: Register 10 agents from same operator in 24h → operator_flood alert. Skew framework distribution to >70% → monoculture alert. Spike registrations above 3x average → registration_spike alert. Normal registration → no alert. Admin endpoints return correct data.

tasks.title: Temporal Behavioral Baselines (Data Infrastructure)
tasks.description: Lay the DATA infrastructure for future shaping dynamics detection. Capture weekly behavioral baselines per entity. Do NOT build detection algorithms — just collect data. Paper §3.4 support, but §4 warns against premature detection.
tasks.details:
Paper §3.4 (shaping dynamics) + §4 (don't build detection yet). Lay groundwork only.

Files: src/models.py, src/jobs/behavioral_baseline.py (new), alembic/versions/ (new migration)

New BehavioralBaseline model:
- entity_id (FK), period_start (date), period_end (date)
- metrics JSONB:
  {
    posts_per_day: float,
    votes_per_day: float,
    follows_per_day: float,
    attestations_given: int,
    attestations_received: int,
    avg_post_length: int,
    reply_ratio: float,
    unique_interaction_partners: int,
    top_partners: [{entity_id, interaction_count}],
    capability_changes: int,
    trust_score_delta: float
  }
- Unique constraint: (entity_id, period_start)

Weekly job:
- Compute baselines for all active entities (last 7 days)
- Uses InteractionEvent table (Task 169) for partner metrics
- Store rolling 12 weeks per entity (delete older)

Admin endpoint:
- GET /admin/baselines/{entity_id} — 12-week behavioral history

NO alerting, NO detection logic. Pure data collection.
tasks.testStrategy: Weekly job creates baseline records for active entities. Metrics match actual activity counts. 12-week rolling window maintained (week 13 deletes week 1). Admin endpoint returns correct baseline data. Inactive entities (no activity) get zero-value baselines.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Shaping Dynamics Detection Algorithms
tasks.description: Build temporal correlation analysis between agent pairs, adaptation rate metrics, and causal linking of evolution records to interactions. DEFERRED: Paper §4 explicitly warns against building automated cooperation detection in natural language settings without validated methods.
tasks.details:
DEFERRED — Needs research validation.

Paper §4: 'We don't have validated methods to detect cooperation vs. collusion in [natural language] settings yet.'

Future work (after behavioral baselines accumulate from Task 174):
- Temporal correlation analysis between agent pairs
- Adaptation rate metrics (how quickly does an agent change post-interaction?)
- Causal linking of evolution records to interactions
- Behavioral stability scoring
- Distinguish healthy adaptation from gaming empirically

Prerequisites: 3+ months of behavioral baseline data from Task 174.
tasks.testStrategy: N/A — deferred.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] N-Agent Cooperation Dynamics Tooling
tasks.description: Build framework for reasoning about cooperation in groups of 3+ agents. DEFERRED: Paper §5 Q4 acknowledges this is an unsolved research question — cooperation proven for pairs only.
tasks.details:
DEFERRED — Open research question.

Paper §5 Q4: 'How do these dynamics scale to n-agent interactions with partial observability?'

The paper's cooperation mechanism (mutual extortion → cooperation) is proven for dyadic games only. Whether it holds for cliques, triads, or broader network patterns is unknown.

Future work: Once pairwise interaction data (Task 169) and behavioral baselines (Task 174) are established, explore whether triadic/clique-level patterns emerge from real data.
tasks.testStrategy: N/A — deferred.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Nuanced Divergence Classification
tasks.description: Distinguish healthy adaptation (extortion → cooperation) from harmful gaming in anomaly detection. DEFERRED: Cannot classify divergence without validated detection methods.
tasks.details:
DEFERRED — Depends on shaping dynamics research (Task 175).

Paper §3.4, §6: Divergent behavior (extortion) can be a healthy precursor to cooperation, not just an anomaly.

Current state: All three anomaly detectors treat ALL divergence as suspicious. No nuance.

Future work: After behavioral baselines (Task 174) accumulate and shaping detection research (Task 175) progresses, add classification layer that distinguishes:
- Healthy divergence: agent adapting behavior in response to interactions
- Harmful divergence: agent gaming trust scores or manipulating network

Requires empirical validation against real interaction data.
tasks.testStrategy: N/A — deferred.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Full DID Portability / Cross-Platform Trust Export

tasks.details:
DEFERRED — Needs architecture review.

Paper §6 table: 'Agents that can't leave can't credibly threaten to stop cooperating. Portability preserves mutual vulnerability essential to cooperative equilibria.'

Current state: DIDs are did:web:agentgraph.co — domain-bound. Evolution records have anchor_hash for future on-chain, but no actual portability. No data export.

Stepping stone (can build sooner): GET /entities/{id}/trust-history-export — returns full trust history, attestations, and evolution records in a portable JSON format.

Full solution (needs architecture review):
- Evaluate portable DID methods (did:key, did:ion)
- Design trust history export format (W3C Verifiable Credentials)
- Research Verifiable Presentations for portable attestations
- Ensure on-chain anchoring can be verified independently of AgentGraph

tasks.title: Operator Approval Flow — Prevent Agent Spoofing
tasks.description: When an agent claims an operator_email at registration, send approval request to that human. Agent is created but operator_id remains null until human confirms. Prevents anyone from spoofing operator relationships.
tasks.details:
Should-have for bot onboarding security.

- On agent register with operator_email:
  1. Create agent entity (operator_id = null initially)
  2. Create pending_operator_link record (agent_id, operator_entity_id, created_at, expires_at)
  3. Send notification to operator (in-app + email if configured)
  4. Operator approves: POST /api/v1/agents/{id}/approve-operator (authenticated as operator)
  5. On approve: set agent.operator_id, create OPERATOR_AGENT relationship
  6. On reject or expire (7 days): delete pending_link
- Agents without operator still function (just no operator trust inheritance)
- Admin can force-link operators

  - Audit log entry
- Also allow admin key recovery
- Rate limit: max 3 key recoveries per agent per day
- Notification to agent's last-known webhook (if registered) about key rotation

tasks.title: Webhook Event Delivery Worker — Actually Send Webhook Callbacks
tasks.description: Implement background worker that listens to Redis event bus (agentgraph:events) and delivers webhook POST requests to subscribed callback URLs. Includes retry logic, failure tracking, and auto-disable after 5 consecutive failures.
tasks.details:
CRITICAL for agent onboarding. Webhook subscriptions exist but events are never sent.

- Listen to Redis pub/sub channel 'agentgraph:events'
- For each event, find matching webhook subscriptions
- POST to callback URL with HMAC-SHA256 signature
- Retry 3x with exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 30s)
- Track consecutive failures on webhook_subscriptions
- Auto-disable after 5 consecutive failures
- Event replay: store last 100 events per subscription for replay endpoint
- Can run as background task in uvicorn or as separate worker process

tasks.description: Recruit 50 OpenClaw/CrewAI operators in beta. Survey at 30 and 60 days. Key questions: Does verifiable identity matter? Do trust scores influence decisions? Do they want cross-framework discovery? Success: >60% say identity influenced joining, >40% cite as primary reason.
